name: Zhuang
notes: Zhuang (Vahcuengh) — Tai language of Guangxi, China. The map commits to STANDARD ZHUANG in the OFFICIAL 1982 LATIN ORTHOGRAPHY (the 1957 alphabet's Cyrillic/IPA-derived letters were replaced by plain Latin letters in the 1982 reform); the pronunciation modelled is that of the standard (Wuming/Shuangqiao) dialect. TONE LETTERS: word-final ⟨z j x q⟩ are TONE letters, not consonants — ⟨z⟩ = ˧˩ (31), ⟨j⟩ = ˥ (55), ⟨x⟩ = ˦˨ (42), ⟨q⟩ = ˧˥ (35); an unmarked (bare) syllable is tone ˨˦ (24), which no letter spells, so it is not in the map. ⟨h⟩ is ambiguous: it is the consonant /h/ in onset but the tone letter ˧ (33) word-finally, handled by a positional_graphemes override; note that in a written compound (e.g. Vahcuengh) an ⟨h⟩ that closes a NON-final syllable is still a tone letter but is only resolvable word-finally by this spec, so it surfaces as /h/ there. OBSTRUENTS: ⟨b d g⟩ are the plain voiceless onsets /p t k/; ⟨p t k⟩ occur only in syllable codas (unreleased stops); ⟨mb nd⟩ are the implosives /ɓ ɗ/; ⟨by gy gv my ngv⟩ are the palatalised/labialised series /pʲ kʲ kʷ mʲ ŋʷ/; ⟨r⟩ = /ɣ/, ⟨s⟩ = /θ/, ⟨c⟩ = /ɕ/, ⟨v⟩ = /w/, ⟨w⟩ = the VOWEL /ɯ/, ⟨y⟩ = /j/. VOWELS: ⟨a⟩ = long /aː/ vs ⟨ae⟩ = short /a/; ⟨o⟩ = /oː/ vs ⟨oe⟩ = /o/; ⟨e i u⟩ = /e i u/. INPUT CONTRACT: 1982-orthography Latin Zhuang text, tone letters written. The 1957 alphabet and the traditional logographic Sawndip script are out of scope; no Sawndip character map is claimed.

ancestors.notes: Tai-Kadai — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Yongbei Zhuang.
